content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 25, 1991


Sydney J. Notkin
1401 El Norte Parkway, #12
San Marcos, CA 92069

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-91-279

Dear Mr. Notkin:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning your responsibilities as a member of the San Marcos
Planning Commission pursuant to the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your
request is general in nature and does not involve a specific
decision, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.²

QUESTION

As a member of the San Marcos Planning Commission, are you
restricted from representing your clients before other city
agencies?

CONCLUSION

Since you will receive income for the purpose of developing a
mobilehome park, you may not participate in any planning
commission decision which will achieve, defeat, aid, or hinder the
completion of the park. Moreover, you may not act or purport to

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-279
Page 2


act on behalf of the planning commission before any member,
officer, employee or consultant of another agency.

FACTS

You are a planning commissioner in the City of San Marcos.
You have been hired by a client to assist in the development of a
mobile home park in your jurisdiction. You anticipate that in the
process of constructing the park your client will need various
city approvals and you may need to appear before the city council
to represent the project of your client. You have asked what
restrictions are imposed on you by the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Act.

ANALYSIS

Planning Commission Decisions

The Political Reform Act was enacted by the people of the
State of California by initiative in 1974. The purpose for the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act was to ensure that
public officials, whether elected or appointed, would perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by
their own financial interests or the financial interests of
persons who have supported them. (Section 81001(b).)

In furtherance of this goal, Section 87100 of the Act
provides:

No public official at any level of state or
local government shall make, participate in making
or in any way attempt to use his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.

As a member of the San Marcos Planning Commission, you are a
"public official" as defined in the Act. (Section 82048.) Thus,
you may not use your official position to influence a governmental
decision in which you know or have reason to know you have a
financial interest.

Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,

Our File No. I-91-279
Page 3


     received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

                         Section 87103(c).

     According to Section 87103(c), any person or business that
has made any payment to you is a source of income to you. If, in
the aggregate, the payment was for $250 or more in the past 12
months, the source is a potentially disqualifying financial
interest.

     You stated that you have been hired by a client to work on
the construction of a proposed mobile home park in your
jurisdiction. Thus, the client is a source of income to you, or
at least promised income, of presumably more than $250.
Consequently, you may not participate in any governmental decision
which will have a reasonably foreseeable³ material financial
effect on your client.

     Moreover, a public official is required to disqualify himself
where there is a nexus between the purpose for which the official
receives income and the governmental decision before the
official's agency. A nexus would exist if the official receives
income to achieve a goal or purpose which would be achieved,
defeated, aided, or hindered by the governmental decision.
(Regulation 18702.1(d).)

     You indicated that you have been hired to work on the project
based upon your expertise in mobilehome park development.
Planning commission decisions will affect the progress of this
project. Under these circumstances, there would appear to be a
nexus between planning commission decisions and your performance
of your private contract.

     Regulation 18702.1(a) provides that the effect of a
governmental decision on an official's economic interests is
deemed to be material and disqualification is required if the


3    Whether the financial consequences of a decision are reasonably
foreseeable at the time a governmental decision is made depends on
the facts of each particular case. An effect is considered
reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial likelihood that
it will occur. Certainty is not required. However, if an effect
is only a mere possibility, it is not reasonably foreseeable. (In
re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198, copy enclosed.)

Our File No. I-91-279
Page 4


source of income is directly before the official's agency,4 or
there is a nexus between the purpose for which you receive income
and the governmental decision before the planning commission.
(Combs Advice Letter, No. A-89-177, copy enclosed.) Consequently,
you must disqualify yourself from participating in planning
commission decisions relating to the project.

    Moreover, you must also disqualify from planning commission
decisions which will have an indirect material financial effect on
your interests pursuant to Regulations 18702.2 and 18702.6 (copies
enclosed). This could occur, for example, if another project
comes before the planning commission which might have a material
financial effect on the mobilehome park project or upon some other
project of your client.

    Participation in governmental decisions has been interpreted
broadly in furtherance of the goals of the Act. Thus, where you
are disqualified with respect to planning commission decisions,
you are also prohibited from making contacts with, appearing
before, or otherwise attempting to influence, any member, officer,
employee or consultant of your agency concerning the governmental
decision. (Regulations 18700 and 18700.1, copies enclosed.)
However, you are not prohibited from preparing drawings or
submissions of an architectural, engineering or similar nature to
be used by a client in connection with a proceeding, and
responding to inquiries from planning staff commission staff
the processing or evaluation of the drawings or submissions,
provided you have no other direct oral or written contact with the
agency. (Regulation 18700.1(b)(4); West Advice Letter, No. A-88-
413, copy enclosed.)

Decisions of Other Agencies

    You stated that to complete the park, your client will be
required to get city council approval. You have asked to what
extent you are prohibited from participating in the presentation
of the project to the city in your private capacity.




4 A source of income is directly before your agency when the
source initiates the proceeding by filing an application, claim,
petition, appeal, or similar request, or is a named party in, or subject
of, the proceeding. A person or business entity is the subject of
a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, renewal,
approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other
approval, entitlement to, or contract with, the subject person or
business entity. (Regulation 18702.1(b), copy enclosed.)

Our File No. I-91-279
Page 5


Regulation 18700.1 provides that even conduct in your private
capacity may be characterized as using your official position to
influence a decision. Regulation 18700.1 provides:

(a) With regard to a governmental decision
which is within or before an official's agency or
an agency appointed by or subject to the budgetary
control of his or her agency, the official is
attempting to use his or her official position to
influence the decision if, for the purpose of
influencing the decision, the official contacts, or
appears before, or otherwise attempts to influence,
any member, officer, employee or consultant of the
agency. Attempts to influence include, but are not
limited to, appearances or contacts by the official
on behalf of a business entity, client, or
customer.

* * *

(c) With regard to a governmental decision
which is within or before an agency not covered by
subsection (a), the official is attempting to use
his or her official position to influence the
decision if, for the purpose of influencing the
decision, the official acts or purports to act on
behalf of, or as the representative of, his or her
agency to any member, officer, employee or
consultant of an agency. Such actions include, but
are not limited to the use of official stationery.

Consequently, so long as you do not purport to represent the
planning commission, you are not restricted in participating
before other city agencies in your private capacity. (See e.g.,
Stout Advice Letter, No. I-88-313; Hineline Advice letter,
No. A-88-149, copies enclosed.)

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

Enclosures

Planning Commission Decisions

Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,

received by or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

Section 87103(c).

According to Section 87103(c), any person or business that has made any payment to you is a source of income to you. If, in the aggregate, the payment was for $250 or more in the past 12 months, the source is a potentially disqualifying financial interest.

You stated that you have been hired by a client to work on the construction of a proposed mobile home park in your jurisdiction. 3 Whether the financial consequences of a decision are reasonably foreseeable at the time a governmental decision is made depends on the facts of each particular case. An effect is considered reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. Certainty is not required. However, if an effect is only a mere possibility, it is not reasonably foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198, copy enclosed.)

source of income is directly before the official’s agency,4 or there is a nexus between the purpose for which you receive income and the governmental decision before the planning commission. (Combs Advice Letter, No. A-89-177, copy enclosed.) Consequently, you must disqualify yourself from participating in planning commission decisions relating to the project.

Moreover, you must also disqualify from planning commission decisions which will have an indirect material financial effect on your interests pursuant to Regulations 18702.2 and 18702.6 (copies enclosed). (Regulation 18700.1(b)(4); West Advice Letter, No. A-88-413, copy enclosed.)

Decisions of Other Agencies

You stated that to complete the park, your client will be required to get city council approval. You have asked to what extent you are prohibited from participating in the presentation of the project to the city in your private capacity.

4 A source of income is directly before your agency when the source initiates the proceeding by filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request, or is a named party in, or the subject of, the proceeding. A person or business entity is the subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the subject person or business entity. Regulation 18700.1 provides that even conduct in your private capacity may be characterized as using your official position to influence a decision. Regulation 18700.1 provides:

Attempts to influence include, but are not limited to, appearances or contacts by the official on behalf of a business entity, client, or customer.
